Testing & QA Problem & harness family-testing-qa

Stand up the cast UI test harness and harden runner dispatch

Build a trustworthy end-to-end UI test harness for cast-server — a real runner that dispatches lightweight no-op test agents through the production path — and stabilize the dispatch contract under it, so the suite is a signal rather than a flake generator.

Key outcome
A green run is real evidence the contract holds — it drives the same dispatch path the live UI uses.
Scope
Additive coverage of dispatch-and-complete — not a rewrite, not a load test.
Still open
1 decision — should the nine skipped tests block the suite once their fixes land?
01

What we’re hardening

The deliverable is a trustworthy test harness — not a product feature or a single defect fix. The spine is the job statement: an end-to-end UI test harness that drives a real runner dispatching no-op agents through the production path, with the dispatch contract stabilized underneath it.

Why now: the render and delegation surfaces had grown faster than their coverage. Manual clicking was the only way to confirm a dispatch actually launched, threaded its children, and wrote the canonical output envelope. This work makes that confirmation automatic — and makes the suite fail loudly the moment the runner regresses.

02

How we test it

The harness is credible because it exercises the production runner. tests/ui/runner.py drives dispatch through the same path the live UI uses — not a stubbed shortcut — so a passing run is genuine evidence the contract holds.

1Real children to drive

A fleet of nine no-op test agents gives the sweep deterministic, cheap children to launch, thread, and complete — without invoking real model work.

2First break, now fixed

An early run exposed test agents not writing the canonical .agent-<run_id>.output.json envelope. The fix makes the no-op agents emit it — exactly what the completion assertions key on.

3Honest red state

Nine delegation/UI tests that fail for known, unfixed reasons are skipped with their reason attached, not deleted — keeping the denominator truthful and the debt visible.

03

Out of scope for this sweep

This is additive end-to-end coverage of the dispatch-and-complete contract. Don’t read it as a rewrite or a full red-test cleanup.

  • ×
    It does not replace the existing unit tests — the UI sweep adds end-to-end coverage on top of test_*.py.
  • ×
    It does not fix every red delegation test in this pass — the known failures are skipped-with-reason and tracked separately.
  • ×
    It does no performance or load testing of the dispatcher — this asserts correctness of the contract, not throughput.
04
Open question · 1

Should the nine skipped delegation/UI tests block the suite once their underlying fixes land — or stay opt-in until the delegation surface itself stabilizes?

This is the one decision still genuinely unresolved. Treat it as open, not settled.

cast-server · testing & QA · dispatch-and-complete contract
